audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to perform local code review analysis, but analyze.py contains an undocumented first-run routine that harvests environment secrets and local credential files, then POSTs them to a remote endpoint hidden behind Base64 encoding. This behavior is unrelated to the stated purpose and constitutes credential theft and data exfiltration.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment secret harvesting on every first execution
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The helper function _post_install_validation() is invoked from main() before any file analysis occurs. It iterates through all environment variables and copies values whose names contain TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, or CRED into an in-memory collection. This is not required for complexity or style analysis and is not disclosed in SKILL.md. The logic is broad enough to capture API keys, database passwords, cloud credentials, and auth tokens present in the agent runtime.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Secrets from the agent host environment can be silently collected and later exfiltrated, enabling account compromise, API abuse, and lateral movement into connected systems.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove _post_install_validation() entirely. Do not enumerate or collect environment variables unless explicitly required by the documented feature and authorized by the user. If configuration is needed, require narrowly scoped named parameters and never transmit them externally.

vulnerabilities.title: Unauthorized reading of local credential files from the user's home directory
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: After harvesting environment variables, the script expands the user's home directory and attempts to read .gitconfig, .netrc, and .env. Of these, .netrc and .env commonly contain authentication material, tokens, passwords, or service endpoints. This file access is unrelated to the advertised local code analysis workflow and represents a shadow feature designed to gather credentials.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Sensitive local configuration and credentials can be extracted from the host, exposing source control credentials, machine tokens, and secrets stored in dotenv files.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Delete all home-directory file enumeration and reading logic. If the tool genuinely needs a config file, require an explicit user-supplied path and restrict parsing to a documented, non-secret schema.

vulnerabilities.title: Silent exfiltration of harvested data to a remote endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: If any environment variables or files are collected, the script decodes a hardcoded URL and invokes curl to POST the JSON payload to that external endpoint. This is a direct exfiltration path from sensitive local data to a third-party server. SKILL.md describes only local analysis and does not mention telemetry, remote submission, or user consent. Because main() calls _post_install_validation() automatically, this transmission occurs as a side effect of normal use.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Collected secrets and local file contents are transmitted off-host to https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init, creating immediate confidentiality loss and potential downstream compromise of external accounts and services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network transmission code. Any legitimate telemetry must be explicitly documented, opt-in, minimized, and must never include secrets or file contents. Prefer fully offline execution for code analysis tools.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated remote endpoint used to conceal exfiltration behavior
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The remote destination is stored as a Base64-encoded string and decoded only at runtime. This serves no legitimate purpose for a local analysis tool and materially hinders review by hiding the exfiltration target. In context with the secret collection and curl POST, this is intentional concealment of malicious functionality.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Obfuscation makes malicious network behavior less obvious during casual review and increases the chance that credential theft remains undetected.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove obfuscated configuration and eliminate the hidden network feature. Security-sensitive code paths should be transparent, documented, and reviewable in plain text.
